name: Sapphire Preferred
effective_cost: Low — the $100 hotel credit alone covers most of the fee

verdict.text: The best $95 travel card — broad 3x categories and a real path to ~2¢/point via transfers

earn_rates.category: Dining, select streaming, online groceries, gas & EV charging, vacation rentals

earn_note: The Jun 2026 refresh folded gas, EV charging, and vacation rentals (Airbnb/Vrbo) into the 3x tier alongside dining, streaming, and online groceries — one of the broadest mid-tier bonus structures around. Travel booked outside the Chase portal only earns 2×.

points.note: CSP lost its old 25% Chase Travel redemption bonus in the Jun 2025 overhaul and — unlike the Reserve — wasn't given a strong Points Boost to replace it (capped around 1.5¢ on hotels, 1.75¢ on select premium-cabin flights). Transferring to airline/hotel partners is still the best play for this card.

transfer_partners.highlight: Same partner list as the Reserve — World of Hyatt, Aeroplan, Virgin Atlantic, Flying Blue, Singapore, and more, mostly at 1:1.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: Cardholders who apply for CSP on or after Jun 15, 2026 transfer to World of Hyatt at a reduced 4:3 ratio. Existing cardholders keep 1:1 through Sep 30, 2026, then move to 4:3 too — a real value cut on the single best partner in the program.

credits.name: Chase Travel Hotel Credit
credits.subtitle: auto-applies · Chase Travel only
credits.description: Statement credit automatically applied to hotel accommodations booked and paid for through Chase Travel, up to $100/year.
tips: Only counts hotel stays booked via the Chase Travel portal — not hotels booked direct or through other OTAs.
Raised from $50 to $100 in the Jun 2026 refresh.

credits.name: Global Entry / TSA PreCheck / NEXUS Credit
credits.subtitle: $120 · every 4 years
credits.description: One statement credit up to $120 every four years after the Global Entry, TSA PreCheck, or NEXUS application fee is charged to the card. Shown here amortized to an annual figure.
tips: New for CSP as of the Jun 2026 refresh — this credit used to be Reserve-only.
Apply for Global Entry, not just TSA PreCheck — it includes TSA PreCheck and covers the same credit.

credits.name: Apple TV Credit
credits.subtitle: 12 months free · activate by Dec 31, 2026
credits.description: A complimentary 12-month Apple TV subscription (not the full Apple One bundle, and not Apple Music) if activated by December 31, 2026.
tips: One-time, limited-time offer — not an ongoing annual credit like the Reserve's Apple TV+/Music benefit.
Already pay for Apple TV? Cancel the paid plan for the free year, but remember to resume payment after it ends.

credits.name: DoorDash DashPass
credits.subtitle: 12 mo free + $10/mo promo
credits.description: Complimentary DashPass membership for 12 months ($0 delivery fees), plus a $10/month statement credit on non-restaurant (grocery/retail) DoorDash orders through Dec 2027.
tips: Activate DashPass through the Chase app before the deadline — it doesn't turn on automatically.
The $10/mo grocery credit is use-it-or-lose-it each month.
DashPass reverts to a paid membership after 12 months unless you cancel it.

protection_note: Punches well above its $95 fee — primary rental coverage and trip cancellation limits that match the $795 Reserve. The gaps are purchase protection ($500/item vs. the Reserve's $10k/claim), a 12-hour trip-delay trigger (vs. 6 hrs on Reserve), and no cell phone protection at all.
rental_note: Primary coverage up to $60,000 — it pays before your personal auto policy. Decline the rental counter's CDW and pay the full rental with this card.

services.name: Lyft bonus
services.detail: 5x total points on Lyft rides through Sep 30, 2027.

services.name: Peloton bonus
services.detail: 5x points on eligible equipment purchases over $150 (up to $5,000 in purchases) through Dec 31, 2027.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Visa Signature — no added cost on purchases made outside the US.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Free to add — no per-card fee, unlike the Reserve's $195
Earns Ultimate Rewards on their spend (into your account)
Covered by the same purchase and travel protections
No lounge access or hotel status (the primary doesn't get these either)

additional_cards.note: Free authorized users are one of CSP's quiet strengths — pooling a partner's or family member's spend into the 3x categories adds up fast at no extra cost.

timeline.text: Existing cardholders' World of Hyatt transfer ratio moves from 1:1 to 4:3, matching new applicants.
3x expanded to gas, EV charging, and vacation rentals; hotel credit doubled to $100; Global Entry credit and Apple TV benefit added; new cardholders get a reduced 4:3 Hyatt transfer ratio.
25% Chase Travel points bonus removed; replaced by Points Boost, but CSP's boost is capped lower than Reserve's (roughly 1.5–1.75¢ vs. up to 2¢).
Major benefits refresh added 5x on the Chase Travel portal, 3x dining/streaming/online groceries, and the original $50 hotel credit.
Card launches with a $95 annual fee (waived the first year) — the fee itself has never changed since.
